title: Systems and methods generating electronic tokens in response to user location
abstract: A system, including: a non-transitory memory; and one or more hardware processors coupled to the non-transitory memory and configured to read instructions from the non-transitory memory to cause the system to perform operations including: determining a first physical location of a user; comparing the first physical location to data indicative of network availability; and in response to comparing the first physical location to data indicative of network availability, generating a first electronic token and a second electronic token for a mobile computing device associated with the user.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A system, comprising: a non-transitory memory; and one or more hardware processors coupled to the non-transitory memory and configured to read instructions from the non-transitory memory to cause the system to perform operations comprising: determining a first physical location of a user; comparing the first physical location to data indicative of network availability; and in response to comparing the first physical location to data indicative of network availability, generating a first electronic token and a second electronic token for a mobile computing device associated with the user. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein the operations further comprise: processing a transaction by the user using the first electronic token; and deleting the second electronic token in response to subsequent movement by the user. 3. The system of claim 1, wherein the operations further comprise: subsequent to generating the first electronic token and the electronic payment token, determining a second physical location of the user; and deleting the first electronic token further in response to comparing the second physical location to the data indicative of network availability. 4. The system of claim 1, wherein generating the first electronic token and second electronic token is performed further in response to determining that the user is approaching a second physical location associated with a transaction history of the user. 5. The system of claim 1, wherein generating the first electronic token and second electronic token is performed further in response to determining that the user is approaching a second physical location associated with a first merchant. 6. The system of claim 5, wherein the first electronic token and second electronic token are specific to the first merchant. 7. The system of claim 1, wherein the operations further comprise: deleting the first electronic token in response to subsequent movement by the user, 

 and wherein deleting the first electronic token further comprises: receiving a message from the mobile computing device indicating deletion of the first electronic token; and releasing a hold on funds associated with the first electronic token. 8. The system of claim 1, wherein the first electronic token comprises: a digital signature of a services provider that issued the first electronic token; and an indication of a pre-authorized amount of the first electronic token. 9. The system of claim 1, wherein the first electronic token comprises: a credit card number. 10. The system of claim 1, wherein determining the first physical location comprises: detecting a reduction in network signal strength; and in response to the reduction in network signal strength, accessing a location application of the mobile computing device and receiving location information from the location application indicative of the first physical location. 11. The system of claim 1, wherein comparing the first physical location to data indicative of network availability comprises: comparing the first physical location to pre-programmed map data correlated with network signal strength data; and wherein generating the first electronic token and the second electronic token is performed further in response to determining that the first physical location is correlated with network signal strength below a threshold. 12. The system of claim 1, wherein comparing the first physical location to data indicative of network availability comprises: comparing the first physical location to a table of pre-programmed locations; and wherein generating the first electronic token and the second electronic token is performed further in response to determining that the first physical location corresponds to a location of the pre-programmed locations. 13. A method comprising: determining a physical location of a user, wherein the user is associated with a mobile 

 computing device running an electronic transaction application; comparing the physical location to data indicative of network availability and to historical transaction data of the user; and in response to the comparing, generating a plurality of electronic tokens for the electronic transaction application. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein comparing the physical location to data indicative of network availability comprises: determining that the user is approaching an area with network availability below a threshold. 15. The method of claim 13, further comprising: paying a merchant by the electronic transaction application, including providing a first electronic token of the plurality of electronic tokens to the merchant; and subsequent to paying the merchant, deleting a second electronic token of the plurality of electronic tokens in response to determining that the user has left an area with network availability below a threshold. 16. The method of claim 13, wherein each of the payment tokens comprises: a digital signature of a services provider that issued the electronic tokens; and an indication of a pre-authorized amount of a respective electronic token. 17. A non-transitory machine readable medium having stored thereon machine readable instructions executable to cause a machine to perform operations comprising: comparing a physical location of a user mobile device to data indicative of network availability; and in response to comparing the physical location to data indicative of network availability, generating a first electronic token and a second electronic token for an electronic transaction application of the user mobile device; paying a merchant with the electronic transaction application by providing the first electronic token to a computing device of the merchant; and subsequent to paying the merchant, deleting the second electronic token from the user mobile device in response to determining an increase in network availability for the user mobile device. 18. The non-transitory machine readable medium of claim 17, wherein the first electronic token comprises: a digital signature of a services provider that issued the first electronic token; and an indication of a pre-authorized amount of the first electronic token. 19. The non-transitory machine readable medium of claim 17, the operations further comprising: determining the physical location of the user mobile device, including: detecting a reduction in network signal strength; and in response to the reduction in network signal strength, accessing a location application of the user mobile device and receiving location information from the location application indicative of the physical location of the user mobile device. 20. The non-transitory machine readable medium of claim 17, wherein generating the first electronic token and the second electronic token is further in response to comparing the physical location of the user mobile device to historical transaction data of the user mobile device.
